chapter_title.en: Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)
text: إِذَا اشْتَرَيْتَ أُضْحِيَّتَكَ وَ وَزَنْتَ ثَمَنَهَا وَ صَارَتْ فِي رَحْلِكَ فَقَدْ بَلَغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ فَإِنْ أَحْبَبْتَ أَنْ تَحْلِقَ فَاحْلِقْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Myhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you buy your sacrificial animal, and weigh is price, and it comes to be among your rides, so the sacrifice has reached its (appropriate) place. Then if you would like to shave off (your head), so shave’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn abu Hamzah who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When you buy your offering animal, measure its price and it becomes as part of your luggage, the offering now has reached its destination, and if you like you can shave your head.’”
